test_cases.input_summary: Evaluating a cross-sectional survey claiming a link between smartphone use and depression in teenagers
test_cases.notes: Correctly identified the causal inference limitation of cross-sectional design. Self-reported screen time weakness was well-explained. Bottom line verdict was clear and directly useful for editorial decision-making.

test_cases.input_summary: Evaluating a small randomised trial (n=40) of a dietary supplement's effect on cognitive performance
test_cases.notes: Sample size concerns were explained with appropriate statistical context. Supplement industry funding was correctly flagged. The distinction between the RCT design strength and the specific implementation weaknesses was well-drawn.

test_cases.input_summary: Evaluating a large observational cohort study on the association between neighbourhood green space and mental health outcomes
test_cases.notes: Confounding by socioeconomic status was correctly identified as the key methodological concern. The selection bias issue (healthier people choose greener neighbourhoods) was well-explained. Bottom line was appropriately cautious.
